{"step_by_step_thinking": "To determine the level of proteinuria required to diagnose severe preeclampsia, we refer to the medical documents provided. Multiple sources specify the proteinuria threshold for severe preeclampsia. Document [20] states that for preeclampsia with severe features, proteinuria must be greater than 300 mg/24 hours or a protein-to-creatinine ratio greater than 0.3. Document [24] confirms that proteinuria is defined as 300 mg or more in a 24-hour urine sample or a protein-to-creatinine ratio of 0.3 or greater. Document [30] also lists proteinuria of 300 mg or more as a diagnostic criterion for severe preeclampsia. These consistent references across multiple documents confirm that 300 mg of protein in a 24-hour urine sample is the threshold for diagnosing severe preeclampsia. Option A (20 mg), B (200 mg), and D (3000 mg) are not supported by the evidence. Therefore, the correct answer is 300 mg.", "answer_choice": "C"}
